name: rasa.module.tasks
description: Portable task-management lifecycle (intake → triage → backlog → active ⇄ blocked → completed), mountable into any domain or orchestrator. Distilled from rasa.domain.code; engineering specifics replaced by a domain-defined done-gate.

rasa.note: First module-kind Element. Mounts into a parent domain or orchestrator and installs a portable task lifecycle. The one domain-specific concern — what 'done' means — is delegated to a project-owned .claude/done-gate.md (seeded here, skip-if-exists). Domains extend via .claude/<domain>-task-rules.md.

element.description: Element-owned task-management files mirrored into the install target's .claude/ per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Refreshed on Element upgrade. The done-gate and the tasks/ ledger are NOT here — they are project-owned (see seed.files).

files.note: The portable, domain-agnostic task lifecycle spine. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade. References .claude/done-gate.md for the verification contract and .claude/<domain>-task-rules.md for domain extensions — it hardcodes neither.
The four category templates (stub / spec / bug / hotfix). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade. Generalized — no src/e2e/PR/build references.
Task-management skills: /task (lifecycle driver), /backlog (read+render), /roadmap (read+render). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.
Describes what this module ships and how it installs. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

seed.description: One-time, project-owned files copied at install only (skip-if-exists), per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. These belong to the consuming project after install and are never overwritten on Element upgrade — the project's done-gate, its task ledger, and the lifecycle scaffold.

files.note: The per-domain verification contract — what 'done' requires. Project-owned; the domain fills it (code = build+test+review; legal = conflict-check + partner sign-off; etc.). Ships with a minimum-honest default + commented domain examples. Placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.
Human orientation to the tasks/ folder: the lifecycle, the dirs, intake vs triage, categories, the done-gate pointer.
Starter pre-triage capture file. Project-owned; appended to over the life of the project.
Starter phase registry — the SOLE source of phase membership. Project-owned.
Lifecycle dir scaffold (bin/init does not implement scaffold.directories, so the dirs ship as seed .gitkeeps). Tracked-but-untriaged tasks.
Lifecycle dir scaffold. Phase-placed, category-assigned tasks.
Lifecycle dir scaffold. In-flight tasks (at most one per worker).
Lifecycle dir scaffold. Parked tasks with an external blocker.
Lifecycle dir scaffold. Terminal state — acceptance criteria met and the done-gate passed.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). NOTE: when this module is co-installed under a parent domain that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path, Lock L-004); in the kernel pull model each Element gets its own holding folder, so no collision. Substitutes the standard {{ELEMENT_*}} / {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer. The tasks/ lifecycle dirs ship as seed .gitkeeps instead (bin/init does not implement scaffold.directories), so this stays empty.
